between_subjects_variables.name: message_condition
between_subjects_variables.description: Different message types: environmental (industry standard), descriptive norm for hotel guests, descriptive norm for same room, descriptive norm for citizens, descriptive norm for gender

dependent_variables.name: towel_reuse_rate
dependent_variables.measurement: Whether guest reused at least one towel during their stay
dependent_variables.coding: 1 = reused at least one towel, 0 = did not reuse any towels

dependent_variables.name: participation_rate
dependent_variables.measurement: Proportion of guests who participated in towel reuse program

gender_distribution.note: Gender distribution not specified in original study

materials.task_description: Hotel guests are asked to participate in a towel reuse program to help conserve environmental resources

environmental.title: HELP SAVE THE ENVIRONMENT
environmental.message: You can show your respect for nature and help save the environment by reusing your towels during your stay.
environmental.description: Industry standard message focusing on environmental protection without descriptive norm

descriptive_norm_guest.title: JOIN YOUR FELLOW GUESTS IN HELPING TO SAVE THE ENVIRONMENT
descriptive_norm_guest.message: In a study conducted in Fall 2003, 75% of the guests participated in our new resource savings program by using their towels more than once. You can join your fellow guests in this program to help save the environment by reusing your towels during your stay.
descriptive_norm_guest.description: Descriptive norm for hotel guests (global norm)

descriptive_norm_room.title: JOIN YOUR FELLOW GUESTS IN HELPING TO SAVE THE ENVIRONMENT
descriptive_norm_room.message: In a study conducted in Fall 2003, 75% of the guests who stayed in this room (#xxx) participated in our new resource savings program by using their towels more than once. You can join your fellow guests in this program to help save the environment by reusing your towels during your stay.
descriptive_norm_room.description: Descriptive norm for same room (provincial norm)

descriptive_norm_citizen.title: JOIN YOUR FELLOW CITIZENS IN HELPING TO SAVE THE ENVIRONMENT
descriptive_norm_citizen.message: In a study conducted in Fall 2003, 75% of the guests participated in our new resource savings program by using their towels more than once. You can join your fellow citizens in this program to help save the environment by reusing your towels during your stay.

descriptive_norm_gender.title: JOIN THE MEN AND WOMEN WHO ARE HELPING TO SAVE THE ENVIRONMENT
descriptive_norm_gender.message: In a study conducted in Fall 2003, 76% of the women and 74% of the men participated in our new resource savings program by using their towels more than once. You can join the other men and women in this program to help save the environment by reusing your towels during your stay.

instructions.participation: If you choose to participate in the program, please drape used towels over the curtain rod or the towel rack. If you choose not to participate in the program, please place the towels on the floor.
instructions.additional_info: See the back of this card for more information on the impact of participating in this program.

back_of_card.text: DID YOU KNOW that if most of this hotel's guests participate in our resource savings program, it would save the environment 72,000 gallons of water and 39 barrels of oil, and would prevent nearly 480 gallons of detergent from being released into the environment this year alone?

instructions.general: You are a hotel guest staying at a hotel that has an environmental conservation program. You will see a sign in your bathroom asking you to participate in a towel reuse program.
instructions.task: Read the message on the sign and decide whether to participate in the towel reuse program by reusing your towels during your stay.
instructions.response_format: Participate (reuse towels) or not participate (place towels on floor)

procedure.description: Hotel guests check into their rooms and find a towel reuse sign in the bathroom. The sign contains one of five different messages (environmental, descriptive norm for guests, descriptive norm for same room, descriptive norm for citizens, or descriptive norm for gender). Guests decide whether to participate in the program by reusing their towels. Room attendants record whether guests reused at least one towel during their first eligible day of stay.

steps.details: Guest arrives and enters their assigned room
Guest notices sign on towel rack in bathroom with assigned message condition

steps.description: Guest reads message and makes decision
steps.details: Guest reads message and decides whether to participate in towel reuse program
Guest uses towels and either reuses them (participates) or places them on floor (does not participate)
Room attendant checks if guest reused at least one towel on first eligible day

key_measures.primary: towel_reuse_rate (proportion of guests who reused at least one towel)

manipulation_check.description: Verify guests saw the sign and understood the program
questions: Did you see a sign about reusing towels?
Did you understand how to participate in the program?
What was the main message of the sign?
